reasoning: The response is strong overall and clearly structured across the 7 required sections. For encryption, it uses a proven family of protocols (X3DH + Double Ratchet + sender keys), explains forward secrecy/post-compromise recovery, and keeps server-side storage to ciphertext plus routing metadata. The main gaps are implementation precision (e.g., curve/key-type details, active malicious-server key-substitution defenses, deeper multi-device/session handling). Architecture is well right-sized for 1,000 users and self-hosting (Go app + Postgres + WebSocket + Docker Compose), though the diagram includes LB/TLS separation that is somewhat unnecessary for a single VPS deployment. Invite design covers generation, hashed token storage, one-time redemption, audit chain, revocation, and quota/replay controls; however, anti-abuse is somewhat basic (limited treatment of transferability, expiry, and stronger rate limiting/binding). Security model is explicit and good on operator visibility, DB exfiltration, and device compromise blast radius, but less complete on active server attacks and richer metadata leakage discussion. Self-hosting guidance is practical (compose stack, DNS/TLS/SMTP, backups), but monitoring/runbook detail is light and restore steps are not deeply specified. Depth is solid with concrete tables and real-time flow, though a few areas remain high-level and there is duplicated text at the end, suggesting minor polish issues.
